blocks.content: Inside the house next door, Dean found Sophie on the porch at dusk.

She was wrapped in the blanket she'd found in the hall closet, the one with the pattern she'd commented on when they arrived — two days ago, a lifetime ago, in a world where Marcus was alive and the worst thing about the weekend was the traffic on Route 12.
He sat beside her and she moved the blanket to cover both of them and they looked at the water.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: Someone in that house killed him, Dean.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: Davenport isn't saying it out loud but you can see it. The way he looks at us. The way Serrano writes things down.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: His job is to figure out which one of us did it.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: I keep looking at everyone and trying to — I don't know. See it. See something that would tell me. And then I feel sick for doing it because these are our friends.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: Do you ever look at me and think—

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: No. Never. Not for a second.
She leaned into him. He put his arm around her. The ocean kept doing its thing.
